default.label: Red / SST
default.med_note: Biotin (Vit B7) supplements >5 mg/day can interfere with immunoassay-based tests. Stop 48h before if possible.
default.diet_note: Avoid heavy exercise 24h before. Stay well hydrated.

data.label: Grey (Fluoride)
data.prep: Patient must fast for 8-12 hours. Water allowed.
data.med_avoid: Avoid insulin/oral hypoglycaemics on the morning of test (take after collection). Steroids, thiazides, beta-blockers elevate glucose.
data.diet_avoid: No food, tea, coffee, juice, or milk for 8-12h. Plain water allowed.
data.diet_note: Avoid heavy exercise or physical exertion the night before.

data.label: Grey (Fluoride)
data.prep: Collect exactly 2 hours after a standard meal. Note meal time.
data.med_avoid: Do not take antidiabetic medications until after the 2-hour sample is drawn unless instructed by physician.
data.diet_note: Eat a standard meal (not heavy/high-fat). Note exact meal time on request form.

data.label: Grey (Fluoride)
data.prep: No fasting required.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: Collect between 8-9 AM. Patient should be awake for at least 1 hour.
data.med_avoid: Exogenous steroids (hydrocortisone, prednisolone, dexamethasone) suppress cortisol. Note all steroid use on form.
data.med_note: Oral contraceptives increase cortisol-binding globulin — note on form.
data.diet_note: Patient must be awake and calm for at least 1h before 8-9 AM draw.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: Collect between 4-6 PM.

data.label: Urine Container
data.prep: Midstream clean-catch urine. First morning preferred for albumin tests.

data.label: 24h Urine Container
data.prep: Discard first morning void. Collect ALL urine for 24h. Refrigerate during collection.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: Avoid prolonged tourniquet. Do not clench fist repeatedly.
data.med_note: Diuretics, ACE inhibitors, ARBs, and laxatives significantly affect electrolytes. Note all medications on form.
data.diet_note: Avoid prolonged tourniquet application and repeated fist clenching — causes pseudo-hyperkalaemia.

data.label: Lavender (EDTA)
data.prep: Transport on ice immediately. Process within 15 minutes of collection. CRITICAL.
data.med_note: Valproate, diuretics, and GI bleeding elevate ammonia. Note on form.
data.diet_note: Avoid high-protein meal 8h before. No smoking 4h before (smoking raises ammonia).
data.diet_avoid: No cigarettes for at least 4h before collection.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: 12 hour fast required. No alcohol 24h prior. Water allowed.
data.med_note: Statins, fibrates, niacin affect lipid results but do NOT stop — note on form. Oral contraceptives raise triglycerides.
data.med_avoid: Avoid alcohol for at least 24-48h before.
data.diet_avoid: No food for 12h. No alcohol 48h before. Water allowed.
data.diet_note: Avoid high-fat meal the previous night. Avoid strenuous exercise 24h before.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: No special preparation needed.
data.med_avoid: Aspirin (even low-dose) raises uric acid. Thiazide diuretics, ciclosporin also raise levels. Note on form.
data.diet_avoid: Avoid high-purine foods (red meat, organ meats, shellfish, beer) 24h before for accurate baseline.
data.diet_note: Avoid alcohol 24h before.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: Avoid heavy exercise before collection. Adequate hydration important.
data.med_note: NSAIDs, ACE inhibitors, aminoglycosides, contrast agents affect creatinine. Note nephrotoxic drugs.
data.diet_avoid: Avoid high-protein meal (especially red meat) 24h before creatinine test — causes transient rise.
data.diet_note: Stay well hydrated. Avoid intense exercise 24h before.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: Fasting preferred but not mandatory. Note all medications.
data.med_avoid: Paracetamol (>2g/day), statins, anti-TB drugs, NSAIDs, herbal supplements can elevate LFTs. Note all on form.
data.diet_avoid: Avoid alcohol for at least 48h before. Avoid fatty meal night before.
data.diet_note: Vigorous exercise raises AST/ALT — avoid 24h before.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: Morning fasting sample preferred. Iron levels show diurnal variation — highest in morning.
data.med_avoid: Stop oral iron supplements 24-48h before serum iron/TIBC. Iron injections: wait at least 1 week.
data.diet_avoid: No food for at least 8h before serum iron. Iron-rich foods (meat, spinach) raise levels transiently.
data.diet_note: Serum ferritin is an acute-phase reactant — elevated in infection/inflammation regardless of iron stores. Note any fever/illness.

default.label: Lavender (EDTA)
default.prep: Mix gently by inversion 8-10 times. Do not shake.
default.med_note: G-CSF/GM-CSF (filgrastim) causes leucocytosis. Corticosteroids cause neutrophilia. Chemotherapy agents suppress counts. Note all on form.
default.diet_note: No special dietary restriction for routine CBC. Stay well hydrated.

data.label: Blue (Citrate 3.2%)
data.prep: Fill tube EXACTLY to mark. Ratio critical (9:1 blood:citrate). Mix gently 4 times.
data.med_avoid: Do NOT stop anticoagulants (warfarin, heparin) — the test monitors them. Note current dose and last dose time.
data.med_note: Antibiotics (especially cephalosporins, metronidazole), aspirin, NSAIDs, and herbal supplements (ginkgo, garlic, fish oil) prolong PT/INR.
data.diet_note: Warfarin patients: maintain consistent Vitamin K intake (leafy greens). Sudden changes in diet alter INR significantly.

data.label: Lavender (EDTA)
data.prep: Must be processed within 1 hour of collection.
data.med_note: Corticosteroids lower ESR. Dextran infusions raise ESR artificially.
data.diet_note: Must be processed within 1 hour of collection — transport promptly.

data.label: Lavender (EDTA) + Slides
data.prep: Prepare thin and thick smear immediately. Collect during fever spike if possible.

data.label: Lavender (EDTA)
data.prep: No fasting required. Reflects 3-month average.
data.med_note: Erythropoietin, iron therapy (when correcting anaemia) lowers HbA1c. Hydroxyurea and dapsone interfere with some assay methods. Note haemoglobin variants (sickle cell, thalassaemia) — affects HPLC method.
data.diet_note: No fasting required. HbA1c reflects 3-month average — single meals do not affect it.

data.label: Lavender (EDTA)
data.prep: Label carefully with patient name and DOB. Critical test.

data.label: Blue (Citrate 3.2%)
data.prep: Fill tube EXACTLY to mark (9:1 ratio critical). Mix gently 4 times. Process within 4h.
data.med_note: D-dimer is elevated in pregnancy, post-surgery, infection, malignancy — not specific for DVT/PE in these conditions. Note clinical context.
data.diet_note: No dietary restriction. Acute test — no preparation needed.

default.label: Red / SST
default.prep: Allow to clot 30 min. Centrifuge before transport.
default.med_note: Biotin supplements >5 mg/day interfere with most immunoassay hormone tests (TSH, FT3, FT4, FSH, LH, prolactin, etc.). Stop biotin for 48-72h before testing.
default.diet_note: Hormone levels show significant diurnal variation. Morning collection standardises results.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: Note menstrual cycle day on request form. Prolactin: avoid stress, collect after 30 min rest.
data.med_avoid: Oral contraceptives, hormonal IUDs, HRT suppress FSH/LH. Note cycle day on form.
data.med_note: Clomiphene, GnRH analogues significantly affect FSH/LH. Note all hormonal medications.
data.diet_note: Note day of menstrual cycle on form — critical for interpretation of FSH/LH/oestradiol.
data.diet_avoid: Avoid high-protein meal within 4h.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: Patient must rest quietly for 30 minutes before collection. Avoid stress, breast stimulation.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: Fasting insulin: 8-12h fast. Transport on ice. Process within 1h.

data.label: Lavender (EDTA) on ICE
data.prep: CRITICAL: Collect in chilled EDTA tube. Transport on ice. Process within 30 min. Collect 8-9 AM.
data.med_avoid: Exogenous steroids suppress ACTH — note all corticosteroid use. Even inhaled steroids in high doses can suppress.
data.med_note: Stress, illness, pain, hypoglycaemia all raise ACTH. Collect when patient is calm and well.
data.diet_note: Patient must be fasting (overnight) and at rest. Collect 8-9 AM after lying supine for 30 min.
data.diet_avoid: Overnight fast required.

data.label: Lavender (EDTA)
data.prep: Note patient posture (upright/supine) and duration on form. Collect after specified posture maintained.
data.med_avoid: ACE inhibitors, ARBs, beta-blockers, diuretics, NSAIDs, oral contraceptives — all affect renin/aldosterone. Ideally stop 2-4 weeks before (only if safe — consult physician).
data.med_note: Spironolactone must be stopped for at least 6 weeks. Calcium channel blockers have minimal effect.
data.diet_avoid: Maintain normal sodium diet 3 days before. Low-sodium diet raises renin; high-sodium suppresses it.
data.diet_note: Note posture (upright vs supine) maintained before and during collection on request form.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: Protect from light after collection.
data.med_note: Current Vitamin D supplementation does not need to be stopped — test reflects true status. Note supplement dose on form.
data.diet_note: Protect sample from light. Obese patients and those with malabsorption syndromes may need higher supplementation — note BMI/history.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: No B12 supplements 24h prior. Protect from light.
data.med_avoid: Stop oral B12 supplements 24h before. If on B12 injections, test before the next scheduled injection.
data.med_note: Metformin, PPIs, H2 blockers reduce B12 absorption over time. Note on form. Folate: stop folate supplements 24h before.
data.diet_note: Vegans/vegetarians are at high risk of B12 deficiency — note on form.
data.diet_avoid: No B12-rich foods (meat, eggs, dairy) are required to be avoided — test reflects stores not recent intake.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: Note LMP on request. Serum more sensitive than urine.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: Note exact time of each sample. Patient should be at rest. Avoid stress.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: Morning collection preferred. No fasting required. Note thyroid medications.
data.med_avoid: Take thyroid medications (levothyroxine/T4) AFTER blood draw for accurate TSH. If testing dose adequacy, take medication as usual and note time of last dose.
data.med_note: Biotin >5 mg/day: stop 48-72h before. Amiodarone, lithium, steroids, dopamine, heparin — all affect thyroid tests. Note on form. Iodine-containing contrast: wait 4-6 weeks.
data.diet_note: No dietary restriction. Avoid iodine-rich supplements (kelp, seaweed) for 1 week before anti-TPO/thyroglobulin tests.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: Fasting insulin: 8-12h fast. Collect on ice. Process within 1h of collection.
data.med_avoid: Do not inject insulin on morning of test. Oral hypoglycaemics: take after collection for fasting insulin.
data.med_note: Steroids, thiazides, oral contraceptives raise insulin resistance. Note on form.
data.diet_avoid: Strict 8-12h fast. No juice, tea, coffee, or milk. Plain water allowed.
data.diet_note: Avoid vigorous exercise 24h before — exercise lowers insulin levels.

default.label: Red / SST
default.prep: Allow to clot 30 min before centrifuge.
default.med_note: Immunosuppressants (steroids, azathioprine, biologics) may suppress antibody titres — note on form.
default.diet_note: No special dietary restriction for most serology tests.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: Best collected after 5-7 days of fever. Note fever duration on form.
data.med_avoid: Do not collect after starting antibiotics — titres may be falsely negative.
data.med_note: Prior typhoid vaccination can give false positive Widal. Recent salmonella infection can cross-react.
data.diet_note: Note fever duration and onset date on request form. Best collected after day 5-7 of untreated fever.

data.label: Red / SST
data.timing: NS1: first 5 days. IgM: after day 5
data.prep: NS1 antigen: collect in first 5 days of fever. IgM/IgG: after day 5.
data.diet_note: Note exact day of fever onset on form. NS1 is most sensitive in first 5 days; IgM rises after day 5.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: Handle as biohazard. Double bag. Label clearly.
data.med_note: Recent HBV vaccination can cause transient HBsAg positivity (rare). Note vaccination date if recent.
data.diet_note: No dietary restriction. Handle as biohazard. Strict universal precautions.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: Note gestational age if pregnant. Larger volume needed for full panel.

data.label: Not applicable
data.prep: Intradermal injection of 0.1 mL PPD on forearm. Read result at 48-72 hours. Mark site.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: No special preparation. Note all immunosuppressive medications on form.
data.med_note: Methotrexate, hydroxychloroquine, biologics (adalimumab, rituximab) significantly affect ANA/RF/complement levels. Note dose and last administration date.
data.diet_note: No dietary restriction.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: Acute phase test — no preparation needed.
data.med_note: Steroids and NSAIDs lower CRP and may mask infection/inflammation. Note on form.
data.diet_note: Obesity causes mild chronic CRP elevation. Recent vigorous exercise also raises CRP transiently.

default.label: Urine Container
default.prep: Midstream clean-catch. First morning specimen preferred.
default.diet_note: First morning midstream urine preferred. Avoid excess Vitamin C supplements 24h before.
default.med_note: Rifampicin turns urine orange-red. Nitrofurantoin, metronidazole cause false positive nitrite. Vitamin C (ascorbic acid) >500 mg/day causes false negative glucose and blood in dipstick. Note all medications.

data.label: Stool Container
data.prep: Fresh specimen within 1 hour. Do not contaminate with urine or water.
data.med_note: Antibiotics may suppress pathogens — collect before starting antibiotics if possible.
data.diet_note: Collect fresh specimen within 1 hour. Do not contaminate with urine or toilet water.

data.label: Sterile wide-mouth container
data.prep: 2-5 days sexual abstinence. Collect by masturbation. Keep at body temperature. Deliver within 30 min.
data.med_note: Anabolic steroids, testosterone therapy, chemotherapy, alpha-blockers, antidepressants (SSRIs), antihypertensives (calcium channel blockers) affect semen parameters. Note all on form.
data.diet_avoid: Avoid alcohol for at least 5 days before. Avoid hot baths, saunas, tight underwear for 2 weeks before (heat affects sperm quality).
data.diet_note: 2-5 days abstinence strictly. Longer abstinence (>7 days) reduces motility.

data.label: Urine Container
data.prep: First morning urine. Most concentrated. HCG highest in morning.

data.label: Stool Container (FOBT)
data.prep: Collect on 3 consecutive days for best sensitivity. Use applicator stick — do not contaminate with urine or water.
data.med_avoid: STOP for at least 3 days before: Aspirin, NSAIDs (ibuprofen, naproxen, diclofenac), iron supplements, Vitamin C supplements >250 mg/day. These cause false positives (aspirin/NSAIDs) or false negatives (Vit C).
data.med_note: Anticoagulants (warfarin, aspirin) may cause GI bleeding — inform physician before stopping.
data.diet_avoid: For guaiac-based FOBT (gFOBT): avoid red meat, liver, processed meats, turnips, horseradish, cantaloupe for 3 days before. These cause false positives. FIT test: no dietary restriction.
data.diet_note: High-fibre diet 2-3 days before is recommended. Stay well hydrated. Confirm with lab whether gFOBT or FIT method is being used.

data.label: Urine Container
data.prep: Midstream clean-catch. Clean genitalia before collection. First morning specimen preferred.
data.med_note: Vitamin C supplements >500 mg/day cause false negative glucose and haemoglobin on dipstick. Rifampicin colours urine orange. Note all medications.
data.diet_avoid: Avoid Vitamin C supplements >500 mg/day for 24h before.
data.diet_note: Stay well hydrated but avoid excessive water intake just before (dilutes sample). First morning most concentrated.

default.label: Culture Swab / Container
default.prep: Collect BEFORE starting antibiotics if possible.
default.med_avoid: Collect ALL culture samples BEFORE starting antibiotics. Even a single dose significantly reduces sensitivity.
default.med_note: Note all antibiotics already given with dose and timing on form.
default.diet_note: Most culture samples require no dietary restriction. Clinical context and prior antibiotics are critical.

data.label: Blood Culture Bottles (Aerobic + Anaerobic)
data.prep: CRITICAL: Strict asepsis. Disinfect skin 2 min. Collect during fever spike or rigors. Inoculate aerobic bottle first.
data.med_avoid: Collect before first antibiotic dose. If already on antibiotics, note on form and use resin-containing culture bottles which neutralise antibiotics.
data.diet_note: Collect during fever spike or rigors for maximum bacteraemia. Two sets from two different sites increases sensitivity.

data.label: Sterile Urine Container
data.prep: Clean genitalia. Discard first stream. Collect midstream in sterile container. First morning specimen best.
data.med_avoid: Collect before starting antibiotics. If on antibiotics, note on form — sensitivity will be reduced.
data.diet_note: First morning midstream specimen. Clean genitalia with sterile wipe. Do not refrigerate >2h — transport immediately.

data.label: Sterile Sputum Container
data.prep: Early morning. Deep cough — not saliva. Rinse mouth with water first. 3 consecutive morning samples for AFB.
data.diet_note: Rinse mouth with plain water (not antiseptic mouthwash) before collection. Deep cough — must be sputum not saliva. Early morning specimen most concentrated.
data.diet_avoid: Do not use antiseptic mouthwash before collection — kills organisms.

data.label: Special TB tube / Sterile container
data.prep: Early morning specimen. For QFT: collect in 3 specific tubes (nil, antigen, mitogen). Process within 16h.
data.med_note: Note current anti-TB medications and duration of treatment. Genexpert/PCR is less affected by prior treatment than culture.
data.diet_note: Three consecutive early morning specimens significantly increase AFB detection sensitivity.

data.label: Sterile Swab / Container
data.prep: Aspirate preferred over swab. Clean wound surface first. Collect from deepest part.

default.label: Fixative / CytoLyt
default.diet_note: Fix specimen immediately. Delay in fixation causes drying artefact and unsatisfactory smear.

data.label: LBC Vial (CytoLyt/PreservCyt)
data.prep: No intercourse/douching 48h prior. Not during menstruation. Broom device in vial — swirl 10 times.
data.med_note: Vaginal oestrogen cream or pessaries: stop 48h before. Antifungal vaginal preparations: stop 48h before.
data.diet_avoid: No intercourse, douching, or vaginal medication 48h before. Not during menstruation.
data.diet_note: Mid-cycle collection (day 14) preferred for best cellularity. Bring previous Pap/LBC reports.

data.label: Slides + Fixative
data.prep: Prepare smear immediately. Air-dry one slide, fix other in 95% alcohol. Label both.
data.med_avoid: Anticoagulants (warfarin, heparin, clopidogrel, aspirin): ideally stop 5-7 days before aspiration to reduce haematoma risk. Consult physician before stopping.
data.diet_note: No fasting required for superficial FNAC. Deep aspirations may require local protocol.

data.label: Sterile Container + EDTA
data.prep: Send entire specimen. Process within 1 hour. Also send EDTA aliquot for cell count.

default.label: 10% Formalin Container
default.prep: Place in 10% formalin immediately. Volume of formalin must be 10x tissue volume. Label container, not lid.
default.med_note: Prior radiotherapy/chemotherapy causes tissue changes that must be noted on form. Note all neoadjuvant treatment.
default.diet_note: Volume of formalin must be 10x tissue volume. Label container — never the lid. Include clinical details on form.
default.diet_avoid: Do not use saline or water — use only 10% neutral buffered formalin.

default.label: Lavender (EDTA)
default.prep: Do not freeze. Transport at room temperature. Karyotype: 10 mL preferred.
default.med_note: Most DNA-based tests are unaffected by medications. Note any recent blood transfusion — transfused DNA can contaminate sample for up to 3 months.
default.diet_note: Do NOT freeze sample. Transport at room temperature. Note recent transfusion history.

data.label: Sterile container
data.prep: Procedure sample — send immediately at room temperature. Label with GA and procedure date.

data.label: Streck tube (Cell-free DNA)
data.prep: Streck (cell-free DNA preservation) tube mandatory. Note GA on form. Do not refrigerate or freeze.
data.med_note: Streck tube mandatory — no substitute. Do not refrigerate or freeze. Note exact gestational age and LMP date.
data.diet_note: Recent blood transfusion within 3 months may affect cell-free DNA result. Note on form.

data.label: Not applicable
data.prep: 4-6 hours fasting for abdominal USG. Full bladder needed for pelvic/lower abdomen scan.
data.med_note: Metformin: some protocols require stopping 48h before if contrast is anticipated. Check with radiology.
data.diet_avoid: No food for 4-6h. No carbonated drinks or legumes 24h before (cause bowel gas obscuring view). Gas-forming vegetables (cabbage, cauliflower, beans) to be avoided.
data.diet_note: Full urinary bladder needed for pelvic/lower abdomen — drink 4-5 glasses of water 1h before and do not void.

data.label: Not applicable
data.prep: Full bladder for transabdominal. Empty bladder for TVS. Note cycle day for follicular study.
data.diet_avoid: For transabdominal: full bladder required — drink 1 litre of water 1h before and do not void.
data.diet_note: For TVS: empty bladder. Note last menstrual period date and cycle day for follicular study.

data.label: Not applicable
data.prep: Note exact gestational age. NT scan: 11-13+6 weeks only. Bring previous scan reports.
data.diet_note: NT scan window: 11 weeks 0 days to 13 weeks 6 days strictly. Bring previous scan reports and referral. Partial bladder preferred.

data.label: Not applicable
data.prep: No preparation needed. Remove neck jewellery.

default.label: Not applicable
default.prep: Remove metal objects, jewellery. Inform if pregnant.

default.label: Not applicable
default.prep: Remove metal objects. Inform radiographer if pregnant.

data.label: Not applicable
data.prep: Specific preparation required. Confirm with department at time of booking.

default.label: Not applicable
default.prep: Wear comfortable clothing. Remove chest jewellery.
default.med_note: Continue regular cardiac medications unless specifically instructed otherwise by referring physician.
default.diet_note: Wear comfortable, loose-fitting clothing. Remove metal jewellery from chest area.

data.label: Not applicable
data.prep: Light meal 3h before. No heavy exercise 12h prior. Wear comfortable shoes. Continue regular medications unless told otherwise.
data.med_avoid: Beta-blockers (atenolol, metoprolol, bisoprolol) significantly blunt heart rate response — may need to hold for 24-48h if testing functional capacity. Consult physician. Digoxin causes ST changes — note on form.
data.med_note: Continue anti-anginal medications unless testing for ischaemia — in which case physician decides. Note all cardiac medications with doses.
data.diet_avoid: No food for at least 3h before. Avoid caffeine (tea, coffee, cola) for 12h before.
data.diet_note: Comfortable walking shoes mandatory. Avoid smoking for at least 3h before. Wear loose trousers/track pants.

data.label: Not applicable
data.prep: No bath/shower during recording. Note activities and symptoms in diary. Avoid magnets and metal detectors.
data.diet_avoid: No hot bath or shower during recording. Avoid magnets, electric blankets, and metal detectors.
data.diet_note: Maintain normal activity. Keep diary of activities, symptoms, and times. Avoid electric shavers.

data.label: Red / SST
data.prep: Serial samples at 0, 3, and 6 hours. Mark exact time on each tube. URGENT.
data.med_note: Serial sampling mandatory: 0h, 3h, and 6h. Mark exact time on each tube. Renal impairment causes troponin elevation independent of cardiac cause — note creatinine.
data.diet_note: Urgent test — no preparation. Strenuous exercise and myocarditis also raise troponin.

default.label: Not applicable
default.prep: No bronchodilator 4-6h prior (unless testing response). No smoking 4h prior. Loose clothing.
default.med_avoid: Short-acting bronchodilators (salbutamol): hold 4-6h before pre-bronchodilator test. Long-acting (salmeterol, formoterol): hold 12h. Ultra-long-acting (indacaterol, olodaterol): hold 24h. Anticholinergics (ipratropium): hold 6-8h. Tiotropium: hold 24h.
default.med_note: If testing bronchodilator response, do NOT take bronchodilator — patient must be off it as per above. If testing lung function on treatment, take medications as usual.
default.diet_avoid: No heavy meal for 2h before. No alcohol 4h before.
default.diet_note: No smoking for at least 4h before. Wear loose, comfortable clothing. No strenuous exercise 30 min before.

default.label: Multiple tubes as per package
default.prep: 8-12h fasting for most packages. Water allowed. Bring previous reports.
default.med_note: List all current medications on form — packages include tests that are medication-sensitive (thyroid, lipids, glucose, LFT).
default.diet_avoid: Strict 8-12h fasting for all packages including glucose, lipid, and LFT components. No tea, coffee, juice, or milk. Water allowed.
default.diet_note: Come between 7-10 AM fasting. Bring previous reports. Take regular medications AFTER blood draw unless specifically told otherwise by physician.

hemolysis.msg: Hemolysis-sensitive test. Avoid prolonged tourniquet, difficult venipuncture, vigorous mixing.

timing.msg: Time-sensitive test. Record exact collection time on tube.

fasting.msg: Fasting required (8-12h). Confirm patient has fasted.

ice.msg: Requires cold chain. Transport on ice immediately.

protect_light.msg: Protect from light. Wrap in foil after collection.

order.msg: Collect before starting antibiotics if possible.

PA_PREP.fasting_blood:
OnePLUS Lab - Patient Instructions

Your tests require FASTING:
- Fast for 8-12 hours
- Water is allowed
- Take regular medications with water unless advised otherwise
- Come between 7-10 AM

OnePLUS Ultrasound Lab, Pitampura
011-4248 0101
PA_PREP.urine_routine:
OnePLUS Lab - Patient Instructions

For URINE test:
- Collect first morning urine
- Clean the area before collecting
- Collect midstream (let first stream go)
- Bring in clean container

OnePLUS Ultrasound Lab, Pitampura
011-4248 0101
PA_PREP.urine_24h:
OnePLUS Lab - Patient Instructions

24-HOUR URINE COLLECTION:
1. Day 1 morning: Discard first urine
2. Collect ALL urine for 24 hours in the large container provided
3. Keep container in refrigerator
4. Day 2 morning: Add last urine, bring to lab
5. Note start and end time

OnePLUS Ultrasound Lab, Pitampura
011-4248 0101
PA_PREP.semen:
OnePLUS Lab - Patient Instructions

For SEMEN ANALYSIS:
- Maintain 2-5 days sexual abstinence
- Collect by masturbation in sterile container
- Do NOT use condom or lubricant
- Keep at body temperature
- Deliver to lab within 30 minutes

OnePLUS Ultrasound Lab, Pitampura
011-4248 0101
PA_PREP.usg_abdomen:
OnePLUS Lab - Patient Instructions

For ABDOMINAL ULTRASOUND:
- Fast for 4-6 hours (no food/drinks)
- Water is allowed
- Wear loose, comfortable clothing

For PELVIC/LOWER ABDOMEN USG:
- Drink 4-6 glasses of water 1 hour before
- Do NOT urinate before the scan
- Full bladder is needed

OnePLUS Ultrasound Lab, Pitampura
011-4248 0101
PA_PREP.hormone:
OnePLUS Lab - Patient Instructions

For HORMONE tests:
- Note: For female hormones, mention Day of menstrual cycle
- No special fasting required unless specified
- Come to lab in the morning

OnePLUS Ultrasound Lab, Pitampura
011-4248 0101
PA_PREP.culture:
OnePLUS Lab - Patient Instructions

For CULTURE tests:
- Collect BEFORE starting antibiotics if possible
- For URINE culture: clean area, discard first stream, collect midstream
- Bring to lab immediately
- Keep at room temperature (not refrigerated)

OnePLUS Ultrasound Lab, Pitampura
011-4248 0101
PA_PREP.echo_tmt:
OnePLUS Lab - Patient Instructions

For TMT/STRESS TEST:
- Light meal 3 hours before test
- No heavy exercise 12 hours prior
- Wear comfortable clothes and shoes
- Continue regular heart medications unless told otherwise
- Bring previous ECG/reports

OnePLUS Ultrasound Lab, Pitampura
011-4248 0101
PA_PREP.pft:
OnePLUS Lab - Patient Instructions

For SPIROMETRY/PFT:
- No bronchodilator inhaler 4-6 hours before
- No smoking 4 hours before
- Wear loose, comfortable clothing
- Eat light meal (avoid heavy meal)

OnePLUS Ultrasound Lab, Pitampura
011-4248 0101
PA_PREP.lbc_pap:
OnePLUS Lab - Patient Instructions

For PAP SMEAR / LBC:
- Do NOT collect during menstruation
- No intercourse 48 hours before
- No douching or vaginal medication 48 hours before
- Mid-cycle (Day 14) is ideal

OnePLUS Ultrasound Lab, Pitampura
011-4248 0101
